methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Framework Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Whisper Aero
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Shinkei operates in Consumer Tech at the Series A stage, having raised $30M to date. The valuation exercise relies on a comp universe of three private peers—Daylight Computer, Framework Computer, and Whisper Aero—given the absence of public comparables in this segment.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable method given data constraints, uses total capital raised ($30M) as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage progression. This yields an implied value of $60M. The method is grounded in the observation that Series A companies generally achieve a doubling of valuation by their next institutional funding round, though execution risk and market conditions introduce variance around this central estimate.

The triangulation framework supports a valuation range of $51M (low) to $69M (high), with $60M as the median. This 35% spread reflects the uncertainty inherent in a single-method analysis and the limited visibility into Shinkei's unit economics, customer acquisition costs, and retention metrics relative to the three private comparables. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue trajectory, cash burn rate, and product-market fit depth—factors that would tighten this range materially with additional financial and operational disclosure.
